name: Canning Potatoes (White, Cubed or Whole)
recipeInstructions:
1. For a 7-quart canner load, you'll need approximately 35 pounds of potatoes.
2. For a 9-pint canner load, you'll need about 22-1/2 pounds of potatoes.
3. One 50-pound bag yields 8 to 12 quarts, or about 5 pounds per quart.
4. Select small to medium-size mature potatoes for best quality.
5. Avoid storing potatoes below 45°F as they may discolor when canned.
6. If packing whole, choose potatoes that are 1 to 2 inches in diameter.
7. Wash and peel the potatoes.
8. Place the potatoes in an ascorbic acid solution to prevent darkening.
9. Cut the potatoes into 1/2-inch cubes if desired.
10. Drain the potatoes.
11. Cook the potatoes for 2 minutes in boiling water, then drain again.
12. For whole potatoes, boil them for 10 minutes and drain.
13. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart to the jar, if desired.
14. Fill jars with hot potatoes and fresh hot water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
15. Adjust lids and process according to Table 1 and Table 2.
Table 1: Recommended Process Time for White Potatoes in a Dial-Gauge Pressure Canner
- Style of Pack: Hot
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 35 minutes for Pints, 40 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb.
- Altitudes of 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb.
- Altitudes of 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb.
- Altitudes of 6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb.
Table 2: Recommended Process Time for White Potatoes in a Weighted-Gauge Pressure Canner
- Style of Pack: Hot
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 35 minutes for Pints, 40 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb.
- Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb.
